ofm.title: OnlyMonster and Supercreator are the dominant AI OFM management tools in 2026.
ofm.summary: OnlyMonster claims to have outpaced all competitors during Beta; Supercreator offers full AI account operation when implemented correctly. |

ofm.title: Fanvue has doubled down on AI with a dedicated platform.
ofm.summary: Features include Voice AI for automated fan replies and Analytics AI for account optimization.

ofm.title: AI influencer market is now worth $6-8 billion
ofm.summary: and forecasted to grow through 2026. Multiple guides published in early 2026 confirming the business model is maturing.

ofm.title: OnlyFans tightened AI/deepfake detection in 2026.
ofm.summary: Platform now has explicit policies and detection tools for AI-generated content — transparency with fans is critical.

ofm.title: AI agencies are moving to portfolio models
ofm.summary: — building multiple AI models under one digital talent agency rather than single-character plays.

tools.title: OpenClaw v2026.3.22-beta.1 just shipped
tools.summary: with new plugin SDK, ClawHub-first installs, Matrix support, and stronger security hardening. Major shift toward production-ready reliability.

tools.title: Claude Code released hooks system
tools.summary: for deterministic workflow automation — enforce project rules, automate repetitive tasks, integrate with existing tools. This is the path to reliable agent workflows.

tools.title: ContextEngine plugin interface added
tools.summary: — AI memory can now be freely plugged and unplugged, allowing custom context management. 200+ bugs fixed in this release.

tools.title: ClawHub skill system is live
tools.summary: — native `openclaw skills search|install|update` flows with tracked update metadata. Skills now first-class citizens.

market.title: Puerto Escondido real estate accelerating hard.
market.summary: La Punta transforming with boutique developments; United Airlines added Houston route April 2025; more US/Canada routes planned for 2026.

market.title: Digital nomad infrastructure deepening in PE.
market.summary: Coworking expansion, improved internet, long-stay demand becoming "most valuable occupancy stabilizer" for rentals.

market.title: Tulum ATV competition still fragmented.
market.summary: tulumatvrental.com claims "no competitors" (aggressive positioning); tulum-tours.com offering $55 USD rentals. Lagoon Company's SEO holding strong. |

market.title: Earth USA 2026 conference
market.summary: — September 18-20 in Santa Fe, NM. Adobe, rammed earth, compressed earth block focus. Good signal for Beno's construction timing.

market.title: Los Tamarindos (PE area) infrastructure upgrades announced
market.summary: — water system, road paving, drainage improvements for 2025-2026. Municipality investing in growth zones.

macro.risk_summary: Iran war active. Strait of Hormuz closed. 15M bbl/day supply deficit. Global oil crisis.

metrics.label: Brent Crude
metrics.note: Peaked $120 on Hormuz closure (Mar 4). Currently $97-102 on ceasefire hopes. Pre-war: $72.

metrics.label: USD/MXN
metrics.note: Peso weakening. Range: 17.10-18.09 past month. Oil shock pressure + US slowdown fears.

metrics.label: Mexico Inflation
metrics.note: Jumped from 4.02% in Feb. Agricultural inflation at 9.69%. Energy + food driving acceleration.

metrics.label: Gasoline (MX)
metrics.note: Government absorbing via IEPS tax reduction. Cannot sustain if oil stays >100. Diesel also ~26 MXN/L.

metrics.label: Tourism Demand
metrics.note: Tulum airport hit 2M+ passengers. CUN projected 22M annual. But expensive flights = lagging risk in 4-8 weeks.

metrics.label: Geopolitical Risk
metrics.note: US-Israel vs Iran. Hormuz closed since Mar 4. Countries rationing fuel. Global recession risk rising.

geopolitical.title: Strait of Hormuz closed since March 4 -- 15M bbl/day stranded
geopolitical.summary: Countries implementing fuel rationing, shorter work weeks, AC bans. Daily supply deficit of ~15M barrels. QatarEnergy declared force majeure on all LNG exports.

geopolitical.title: Oil execs warn of long-term damage as energy markets "dislocated"
geopolitical.summary: TotalEnergies CEO calls market "dislocated." BNP Paribas raised 2026 inflation forecast to 3.2%. Shell and others warning of structural damage to supply chains.

geopolitical.title: Mexico positioned as alternative crude supplier to Asia
geopolitical.summary: Mexican crude attractive to Asian buyers as Middle East supply cut. Upside: more export revenue. Downside: domestic fuel imports from US still expensive.

geopolitical.title: Ceasefire talks causing oil volatility -- Brent swung $20 in 48 hours
geopolitical.summary: Trump signaling Iran talks. Tehran denying. Brent dropped from $112 to $99 Monday, back to $101 today. Extreme volatility = planning nightmare.

macro.bottom_line: RED ALERT for planning. Oil crisis is real but Mexico is partially shielded (own production + govt subsidies). Lagoon impact: fuel costs up ~15-20% effective, but tourism still strong SHORT TERM. The lag risk hits in 4-8 weeks when expensive flights reduce bookings. Action: track weekly bookings vs last year, hold prices for now, build cash reserves. For Oaxaca build: construction material transport costs rising with diesel. Not urgent but factor into timeline. The peso weakening actually HELPS Lagoon since you price in USD.
